message:
fix(mvcc,repair): bounds-check resolve_data_with index and prevent block address overflow

Two defensive hardening fixes:

1. ffs-mvcc/compression.rs: Add an early bounds check in
   resolve_data_with() — if the caller-supplied index is >= chain.len(),
   return None immediately instead of indexing out of bounds. This
   prevents a panic when a stale or corrupted version index is passed
   through the MVCC resolution chain.

2. ffs-repair/codec.rs: Replace bare arithmetic (first_block.0 + i)
   with checked_add() in both encode_group() and decode_group(). If a
   block group starts near u64::MAX and the offset would overflow, we
   now return a descriptive FfsError::RepairFailed instead of wrapping
   silently, which would cause reads/writes to the wrong block address.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
